folio.label: 62v
folio.l: And euericú man hatetú , hire companye

note.notetxt: 658The ink here becomes again lighter, but not the same as Co. It has lost the colour of the brown.ODR

folio.l: ¢ Woo was this kyng´ , whan he this &lbar;re had sayn
But to no wygút he tolde , hise sorwes soore
But of his owen hond , he wroot a gayn
Welcome the sonde of Crist , for euäe more
To me that am now , lerned in his loore
Lord welcome be thy lust , and thy plesaunce
My lust I putte , al in thyn ordinaunce
¢ Kepetú this child , al be it foul or feyre
And eke my wyf , vn to myn hoom comynge
Crist whan hi~ list , may sende me an Eyr
Moore agreable , than this , to my likynge
This &lbar;re he seeletú , pryuely wepynge
Whicú to the messanger , was take soone
And fortú he gotú , there is nomore to done
¢ O Messanger fulfilled , of drunkenesse
Stronge is thy breetú , thy lymes faltren ay
And thow bywreyest , alle secrenesse
Thy mynde is lorn~ , æè ianglest as a Iay
Thy face is turned , in a newe aray
There dronkenesse regnetú , in any route
There is no counseil hid , w outen doute
¢ O Donegild , I ne haue , noon englissú digne
Vn to thy malice , and thy tyrannye
And æäfore to the fend , I the resigne
Lat hym endyten , of thy traytorie
Fy mannyssú fy , o nay be god I lye
Fy feendly spirit , for I dar wel telle
Thougú æè heere walk , thy spirit is in helle
¢ This messanger cometú , fro the kyngö a gayn
And at the kynges modres court , he ligút
And she was of this messanger , ful fayn
And pleesed hi~ , in al that euäe she mygút
He drank , and wel his girdel vnderpigút
He slepetú and he fnortetú , in his gyse
Al nygút´ to the Sonne gan aryse
¢ Eft weere hise &lbar;res , stolen euäychone
And countrefeted &lbar;res , in this wyse
The kyngö comaundetú , his Constable a noon
Vp peyne of hangynge , and on hey Iuyse
That he ne shulde suffren , in no wyse
Custance witú Inne his regne , for to abide
Three dayes , and o quarter of a tyde
¢ But in the Same ship~ , as he hire fonde
Hire and hire ©ongö sone , and al hire gere
